chapter_title.en: The Limits of Ablution, Its Order and Reward

text: 105 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ وَ تَمَنْدَلَ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ حَسَنَةً وَ مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ وَ لَمْ يَتَمَنْدَلْ حَتَّى يَجِفَّ وَضُوؤُهُ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ ثَلاَثُونَ حَسَنَةً.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.105 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
'Whoever performs ablution and wipes himself (with a towel) will have a good deed written for him. And whoever performs ablution and does not wipe himself until his ablution dries, Allah (swt) will write for him thirty good deeds.'.
